calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jan-26-2024 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Nov-21-2023 Continued To Feb-21-24 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Or Alternatively, Stay, Action For Improper Forum (Ccp 410.30(A)(2)
Off Calendar Nov-13-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: RE: Defendant Siegfried Group, LLP's ("Siegfried") motion to dismiss or stay due to improper forum.

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel, or Interested Person.

The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Defendant Siegfried Group, LLP's ("Siegfried") motion to dismiss or stay due to improper forum is granted. Plaintiff John Wai worked as a certified public accountant for his former employer, Siegfried, a Delaware Corporation, from 2015 to 2022. (FAC para. 1, 3, 5.) When Wai began work, he and Siegfried executed an employment agreement dated June 30, 2015. (Exh. 1 of the FAC.) This agreement contained a forum-selection clause, titled "Governing Law and Venue," which provided that "Delaware shall be designated the exclusive venue" for "any action under this Agreement, including, but not limited to, any action to challenge or to enforce any provision in this Agreement." (Id.) After he was terminated, Wai sued Siegfried in this court for breach of employment contract and defamation. Under the doctrine of forum non conveniens, a court shall stay or dismiss an action in whole or in part when it finds that the interests of substantial justice demand that the action be heard in another forum. (CCP 410.30.) Where a motion to dismiss is based on a forum selection clause, a court should enforce the forum selection clause absent a showing that enforcement of the forum selection clause would be unfair or unreasonable. (Bushansky v. Soon-Shiong (2018) 23 Cal.App.5th 1000, 1011.) A forum selection clause is reasonable if it has "a logical connection with at least one of the parties or their transaction." (Verdugo v. Alliantgroup, L.P. (2015) 237 Cal.App.4th 141, 147.) Here, this forum selection clause is reasonable. Although Wai worked for Siegfried in its San Francisco office, Siegfried is a Delaware Corporation, such that there is a logical connection between it and the chosen forum. (FAC para. 1, 2; Verdugo, supra, 237 Cal.App.4th at 147.) Further, Labor Code 925 does not render this forum selection clause unenforceable. Labor Code 925 prohibits employment agreement provisions that (1) force employees who primarily reside and work in California to litigate outside of California, or (2) deprive the employee of substantive protections of California law. By its plain language, however, Labor Code 925 only applies to contracts "entered into, modified, or extended on or after January 1, 2017." Here, Wai does not dispute that the employment agreement was signed and entered in 2015. (Exh. 1 of the FAC.) To circumvent Labor Code 925(f)'s limited reach, Wai's counsel argues the agreement was "extended daily, as Wai reported for duty and Siegfried accepted that report." (See Opp. 6:21-27.) Contracts, however, are not "extended" by ongoing performance. This was not an employment contract for a fixed term to be extended at a later date. (Comp. with Ryze Claim Solutions LLC v. Superior Court (2018) 33 Cal.App.5th 1066, 1071-1072 [Labor Code 925 did not apply because employee was fired before the employment contract's extension in 2017].) Thus, Labor Code 925 does not apply. Alternatively, Wai's counsel argues Labor Code 925 is a codification of common law that has always prohibited forum selection clauses. (See Opp. 6:11-19.) Wai's counsel, however, has not identified any such common law to support this position. (See id.) In sum, Wai has not met his burden of showing the forum selection clause is unenforceable. The motion to dismiss is granted.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer. Clerk: Robert Woods. Not Reported. (302/RBU)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-13-2023 Continued To Dec-20-23 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Or Alternatively, Stay, Action For Improper Forum (Ccp 410.30(A)(2)
Off Calendar Oct-27-2023 Continued To Nov-13-23 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:

William Romaine, Esq., Romaine Lokhandwala Law Group, LLP, (559) 625-6020, for Plaintiff.

Eisha Perry, Esq., in court, Duane Morris, LLP, (415) 957-3065, for Defendant.

RE: Defendant's Motion To Dismiss Or Alternatively, Stay, Action For Improper Forum (Ccp 410.30(A)(2).

The court notes that it did not receive a courtesy copy of Plaintiff's opposition, pursuant to local rule, and that it appears to be untimely. Court notes that it did not receive a courtesy copy of Defendant's reply. The court advises counsel that is inclined to adopt its tentative rulings as to Defendant's individual Motions to Strike complaint and Amend the complaint and continue the remaining subject matter. No opposition to the court's tentative ruling is presented.

After additional discussion, the court's tentative rulings as to Defendant's individual Motions as specified above are adopted. The tentative ruling on Defendant's Motion To Dismiss Or Alternatively, Stay, Action For Improper Forum (Ccp 410.30(A)(2) is vacated and is continued to Nov-13-2023 at 9:00 am in Dept. 302, Law & Motion. Counsel for Plaintiff is instructed to deliver courtesy copies of his opposition to the Dept. 302, Law & Motion, on or before close of business on Oct-30-2023.

Judge: Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

MOTION TO STRIKE Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-27-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:

William Romaine, Esq., Romaine Lokhandwala Law Group, LLP, (559) 625-6020, for Plaintiff.

Eisha Perry, Esq., in court, Duane Morris, LLP, (415) 957-3065, for Defendant.

Not argued/contested. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Off calendar. The Perry declaration fails to show that the parties met and conferred "in person, by telephone, or by video conference" in compliance with CCP 435.5. Although the declaration indicates the parties met telephonically before the filing of a motion to strike the initial complaint, CCP 435.5(a) indicates that if "an amended pleading is filed, the responding party shall meet and confer again with the party who filed the amended pleading before filing a motion to strike the amended pleading." The parties are ordered to comply with the code. Siegfried Group, LLP's response to John Wai's First Amended Complaint is now due by December 1, 2023.

Judge: Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-27-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:

William Romaine, Esq., Romaine Lokhandwala Law Group, LLP, (559) 625-6020, for Plaintiff.

Eisha Perry, Esq., in court, Duane Morris, LLP, (415) 957-3065, for Defendant.

RE: DEFENDANT SIEGFRIED GROUP, LLC, A DELAWARE CORPORATION DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT.

Not argued/contested. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Off calendar. The Perry declaration fails to show that the parties met and conferred "in person, by telephone, or by video conference" in compliance with CCP 430.41. Although the declaration indicates the parties met telephonically before the filing of a demurrer to the initial complaint, CCP 430.41 indicates that if "an amended complaint, cross-complaint, or answer is filed, the responding party shall meet and confer again with the party who filed the amended pleading before filing a demurrer to the amended pleading." The parties are ordered to comply with the code. Siegfried Group, LLP's response to John Wai's First Amended Complaint is now due by December 1, 2023.

Judge: Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Not Reported. (302/JMQ)
